author: Puko
name: FP3D
shortDescription: Adds functions to position 3D objects and apply forces to them.
description: - applying impulses and forces to objects with physics
- "sticking" objects to other objects or in front of the camera
- translating the normal of an intersection from a raycast into angles (useful for portals or wall jumps!)
- translating the 3D position onto a 2D plane with optional functions such as scaling down or making the icon orbit the screen if the target position is behind the camera
- behaviors that automate or facilitate processes, like SnapToGrid or FPS Parenting.
- condition that checks whether an object or the camera is looking at a target.
= function to get the object closest to the center of the camera (useful for aim assistance)

eventsFunctions.description: Applies an instantaneous 3D physical force (impulse) to an object using spherical angles (2D direction and 3D elevation).
eventsFunctions.name: Impulse
eventsFunctions.sentence: Apply an impulse with force _PARAM5_ at the object _PARAM1_ at the angle _PARAM3_ and elevation _PARAM4_ with physics

eventsFunctions.description: Applies an instantaneous 3D physical force (impulse) to an object towards an position.
eventsFunctions.name: ImpulseTowardsPosition
eventsFunctions.sentence: Apply impulse at _PARAM1_ with force _PARAM3_ towards position _PARAM5_;_PARAM6_;_PARAM7_

parameters: FP3D::Distance(Object.CenterX(), Object.CenterY(), Object.Object3D::CenterZ(), PositionX, PositionY, PositionZ)

eventsFunctions.description: Applies an instantaneous physical impulse at a specific point in 3D space. Because the force can be applied away from the object's center of mass, this function can generate both linear movement and angular velocity (spinning/torque).
eventsFunctions.name: ImpulseAtPoint
eventsFunctions.sentence: Apply an impulse with force _PARAM5_ at the object _PARAM1_ at the angle _PARAM3_ and elevation _PARAM4_ with physics at point _PARAM7_, _PARAM8_, _PARAM9_

parameters.description: The object to which the impulse will be applied.
parameters.name: Object

parameters.description: The X coordinate of the impact point in world space.
parameters.name: PointX

parameters.description: The Y coordinate of the impact point in world space.
parameters.name: PointY

parameters.description: The Z coordinate of the impact point in world space.
parameters.name: PointZ

eventsFunctions.description: Apply an continuous force at the center of object.
eventsFunctions.name: ForceLinear
eventsFunctions.sentence: Apply an linear force _PARAM5_ at the object _PARAM1_ at the angle _PARAM3_ and elevation _PARAM4_

eventsFunctions.description: Applies a continuous 3D force to the object's center towards a specific position.
eventsFunctions.fullName: Apply Linear Force Towards an Position
eventsFunctions.name: ForceLinearTowardsPosition
eventsFunctions.sentence: Apply a force of _PARAM3_ to _PARAM1_ towards _PARAM5_, _PARAM6_, _PARAM7_

parameters: FP3D::Distance(Object.CenterX(), Object.CenterY(), Object.Object3D::CenterZ(), PositionX, PositionY, PositionZ)

parameters.description: The object that will receive the force.
parameters.name: Object

parameters.description: The magnitude of the force (acceleration depends on object mass).
parameters.name: force

eventsFunctions.description: Apply forces to an object changing its linear velocity.
eventsFunctions.name: ForceLinearPhysics

yes = adding force
no = constant force

eventsFunctions.description: Calculates and give you the exact X, Y, or Z coordinate of a point relative to a target object, accounting for that object's full 3D rotation. This is perfect for finding where a "gun barrel" or "attachment point" should be in 3D space, even as the object tilts and turns.
eventsFunctions.name: AttatchPos

inlineCode: // using javascript cause gdevelop limited me to not add behaviors parameters that would allow me compare 3D values

Target.Y() + (Distance * (sin(ToRad(Target.Angle())) * cos(ToRad(Target.Object3D::RotationY())))) + (HorizontalOffset * (sin(ToRad(Target.Angle())) * sin(ToRad(Target.Object3D::RotationY())) * sin(ToRad(Target.Object3D::RotationX())) + cos(ToRad(Target.Angle())) * cos(ToRad(Target.Object3D::RotationX())))) + (VerticalOffset * (sin(ToRad(Target.Angle())) * sin(ToRad(Target.Object3D::RotationY())) * cos(ToRad(Target.Object3D::RotationX())) - cos(ToRad(Target.Angle())) * sin(ToRad(Target.Object3D::RotationX()))))

Target.Object3D::Z() - (Distance * sin(ToRad(Target.Object3D::RotationY()))) + (HorizontalOffset * cos(ToRad(Target.Object3D::RotationY())) * sin(ToRad(Target.Object3D::RotationX()))) + (VerticalOffset * cos(ToRad(Target.Object3D::RotationY())) * cos(ToRad(Target.Object3D::RotationX())))

eventsFunctions.description: Coordinates an object's position and rotation to match the camera's view. It allows for 3D offsets (Horizontal, Vertical, and Distance) and provides smooth interpolation for both movement and rotation to simulate "viewmodel sway" or "weight."
eventsFunctions.fullName: Positioning an object relative to the camera Pos and Angle
eventsFunctions.name: PositioningCamera
eventsFunctions.sentence: Position object _PARAM1_ at the center of camera with distance _PARAM4_, with Xoffset _PARAM3_, Yoffset _PARAM2_, with lerp (float) _PARAM6_ and follow Angles? _PARAM7_; angular lerp _PARAM8_.angles offsets: angle _PARAM9_, Yelevation: _PARAM10_, XElevation: _PARAM11_.Enables YRotation? _PARAM12_ Enables XRotation? _PARAM13_ at layer: _PARAM14_

parameters.description: Local Y-axis offset relative to camera center.
parameters.name: VerticalOffset

parameters.description: Local X-axis offset relative to camera center.
parameters.name: HorizontalOffset

parameters.description: Toggle to make the object match camera rotation.
parameters.name: FollowAngles

eventsFunctions.description: Positions a follower object relative to a target object's 3D position and rotation. It translates local offsets into global world coordinates, allowing the follower to maintain its relative position even as the target moves and rotates in three dimensions.
eventsFunctions.fullName: Positioning an Object in front of Another
eventsFunctions.name: PositioningObject

Enables YRotation? _PARAM12_ Enables XRotation? _PARAM13_. from _PARAM16_

parameters: Target.X() + (-Distance * (cos(ToRad(Target.Angle())) * cos(ToRad(Target.Object3D2::RotationY())))) + (HorizontalOffset * (cos(ToRad(Target.Angle())) * sin(ToRad(Target.Object3D2::RotationY())) * sin(ToRad(Target.Object3D2::RotationX())) - sin(ToRad(Target.Angle())) * cos(ToRad(Target.Object3D2::RotationX())))) + (VerticalOffset * (cos(ToRad(Target.Angle())) * sin(ToRad(Target.Object3D2::RotationY())) * cos(ToRad(Target.Object3D2::RotationX())) + sin(ToRad(Target.Angle())) * sin(ToRad(Target.Object3D2::RotationX()))))

Target.Y() + (-Distance * (sin(ToRad(Target.Angle())) * cos(ToRad(Target.Object3D2::RotationY())))) + (HorizontalOffset * (sin(ToRad(Target.Angle())) * sin(ToRad(Target.Object3D2::RotationY())) * sin(ToRad(Target.Object3D2::RotationX())) + cos(ToRad(Target.Angle())) * cos(ToRad(Target.Object3D2::RotationX())))) + (VerticalOffset * (sin(ToRad(Target.Angle())) * sin(ToRad(Target.Object3D2::RotationY())) * cos(ToRad(Target.Object3D2::RotationX())) - cos(ToRad(Target.Angle())) * sin(ToRad(Target.Object3D2::RotationX()))))

Target.X() + (-Distance * (cos(ToRad(Target.Angle())) * cos(ToRad(Target.Object3D2::RotationY())))) + (HorizontalOffset * (cos(ToRad(Target.Angle())) * sin(ToRad(Target.Object3D2::RotationY())) * sin(ToRad(Target.Object3D2::RotationX())) - sin(ToRad(Target.Angle())) * cos(ToRad(Target.Object3D2::RotationX())))) + (VerticalOffset * (cos(ToRad(Target.Angle())) * sin(ToRad(Target.Object3D2::RotationY())) * cos(ToRad(Target.Object3D2::RotationX())) + sin(ToRad(Target.Angle())) * sin(ToRad(Target.Object3D2::RotationX()))))

Target.Y() + (-Distance * (sin(ToRad(Target.Angle())) * cos(ToRad(Target.Object3D2::RotationY())))) + (HorizontalOffset * (sin(ToRad(Target.Angle())) * sin(ToRad(Target.Object3D2::RotationY())) * sin(ToRad(Target.Object3D2::RotationX())) + cos(ToRad(Target.Angle())) * cos(ToRad(Target.Object3D2::RotationX())))) + (VerticalOffset * (sin(ToRad(Target.Angle())) * sin(ToRad(Target.Object3D2::RotationY())) * cos(ToRad(Target.Object3D2::RotationX())) - cos(ToRad(Target.Angle())) * sin(ToRad(Target.Object3D2::RotationX()))))

eventsFunctions.description: Moves a physics-enabled object to a relative position in front of the camera by manipulating its Linear Velocity. This creates a "magnetic" follow effect where the object smoothly trails the camera but remains a physical entity that can interact with the environment.
eventsFunctions.fullName: Positioning an object relative to the camera Pos and Angle(Physics)
eventsFunctions.name: PositioningCameraPhysics
eventsFunctions.sentence: Position object _PARAM1_ at the center of camera with distance _PARAM2_, with Xoffset _PARAM3_, Yoffset _PARAM4_, with lerp (float) _PARAM7_ with Physics, at layer: _PARAM8_

parameters: ((CameraCenterX(Layer) + (-Distance * cos(ToRad(CameraAngle(Layer) - 90)) * cos(ToRad(Scene3D::CameraRotationX(Layer) - 90))) + (HorizontalOffset * cos(ToRad(CameraAngle(Layer)))) + (VerticalOffset * cos(ToRad(CameraAngle(Layer) - 90)) * sin(ToRad(Scene3D::CameraRotationX(Layer) - 90)))) - Object.CenterX()) * lerp

((CameraCenterY(Layer) + (-Distance * sin(ToRad(CameraAngle(Layer) - 90)) * cos(ToRad(Scene3D::CameraRotationX(Layer) - 90))) + (HorizontalOffset * sin(ToRad(CameraAngle(Layer)))) + (VerticalOffset * sin(ToRad(CameraAngle(Layer) - 90)) * sin(ToRad(Scene3D::CameraRotationX(Layer) - 90)))) - Object.CenterY()) * lerp

parameters.description: 2D pos in X Axis, 0 is center
parameters.name: HorizontalOffset

parameters.description: 2D pos in Y Axis, 0 is center
parameters.name: VerticalOffset

It translates local offsets into global world coordinates, allowing the follower to maintain its relative position even as the target moves and rotates in three dimensions.
eventsFunctions.fullName: Positioning an Object in front of Another (Physics)
eventsFunctions.name: PositioningPhysics
eventsFunctions.sentence: Position object _PARAM1_ in front of _PARAM14_ with distance _PARAM4_, with Xoffset _PARAM3_, Yoffset _PARAM2_, with strength _PARAM6_ and follow Angles? _PARAM7_; angular lerp _PARAM8_. angles offsets: angle _PARAM9_, Yelevation: _PARAM10_, XElevation: _PARAM11_. Enables YRotation? _PARAM12_ Enables XRotation? _PARAM13_. from _PARAM16_

parameters: ((Target.CenterX() + (-Distance * cos(ToRad(CameraAngle() - 90)) * cos(ToRad(Scene3D::CameraRotationX() - 90))) + (HorizontalOffset * cos(ToRad(CameraAngle()))) + (VerticalOffset * cos(ToRad(CameraAngle() - 90)) * sin(ToRad(Scene3D::CameraRotationX() - 90)))) - Object.CenterX()) * Strength

((Target.CenterY() + (-Distance * sin(ToRad(CameraAngle() - 90)) * cos(ToRad(Scene3D::CameraRotationX() - 90))) + (HorizontalOffset * sin(ToRad(CameraAngle()))) + (VerticalOffset * sin(ToRad(CameraAngle() - 90)) * sin(ToRad(Scene3D::CameraRotationX() - 90)))) - Object.CenterY()) * Strength

parameters.description: 2D pos in X Axis, 0 is center
parameters.name: HorizontalOffset

parameters.description: 2D pos in Y Axis, 0 is center
parameters.name: VerticalOffset

eventsFunctions.description: Constrains the position of one object relative to another based on a minimum distance, a maximum distance, and an angular "cone" limit based on the first object's orientation.
eventsFunctions.name: DistanceConstraint
eventsFunctions.sentence: Apply a constraint of  to _PARAM3_ relative to _PARAM1_ (Range: _PARAM5_ to _PARAM6_, Max Angle: _PARAM7_°)

parameters: sqrt(pow(Object1.CenterX() - Object2.CenterX(), 2) + pow(Object1.CenterY() - Object2.CenterY(), 2) + pow(Object1.Object3D::CenterZ() - Object2.Object3D1::CenterZ(), 2))
Object1.CenterX() + (fwdx * cos(ToRad(AngleLimit)) + (dX - fwdx * dt) / max(0.0001, sin(a)) * sin(ToRad(AngleLimit))) * CuDist
Object1.CenterY() + (fwdy * cos(ToRad(AngleLimit)) + (dy - fwdy * dt) / max(0.0001, sin(a)) * sin(ToRad(AngleLimit))) * CuDist
Object1.Object3D::CenterZ() + (fwdz * cos(ToRad(AngleLimit)) + (dz - fwdz * dt) / max(0.0001, sin(a)) * sin(ToRad(AngleLimit))) * CuDist

parameters.description: The closest the object is allowed to get.
parameters.name: MinDistance

parameters.description: The furthest Object is allowed to stray.
parameters.name: MaxDistance

parameters.description: The maximum angle (in degrees) Object2 can deviate from the forward facing direction of Object parent.
parameters.name: AngleLimit

eventsFunctions.description: Uses the 3D Physics engine to apply impulses that keep an object within a specific spherical and angular range of another object. Because it uses forces, the movement is fluid and respects the object's mass and velocity.
eventsFunctions.fullName: Apply 3D Physics-based Distance and Angle Constraint
eventsFunctions.name: DistanceConstraintPhysics
eventsFunctions.sentence: Apply a physical constraint of _PARAM9_ strength to _PARAM3_ relative to _PARAM1_ (Range: _PARAM5_ to _PARAM6_, Max Angle: _PARAM7_°)

parameters: sqrt(pow(Object1.CenterX() - Object2.CenterX(), 2) + pow(Object1.CenterY() - Object2.CenterY(), 2) + pow(Object1.Object3D::CenterZ() - Object2.Object3D1::CenterZ(), 2))
((Object1.CenterX() + (fwdx * cos(ToRad(AngleLimit)) + (dX - fwdx * dt) / max(0.0001, sin(a)) * sin(ToRad(AngleLimit))) * CuDist) - Object2.CenterX()) * Strength
((Object1.CenterY() + (fwdy * cos(ToRad(AngleLimit)) + (dy - fwdy * dt) / max(0.0001, sin(a)) * sin(ToRad(AngleLimit))) * CuDist) - Object2.CenterY()) * Strength
((Object1.Object3D::CenterZ() + (fwdz * cos(ToRad(AngleLimit)) + (dz - fwdz * dt) / max(0.0001, sin(a)) * sin(ToRad(AngleLimit))) * CuDist) - Object2.Object3D1::CenterZ()) * Strength
((Object1.CenterX() + ((Object2.CenterX() - Object1.CenterX()) / max(0.001, CuDist)) * MaxDistance) - Object2.CenterX()) * Strength
((Object1.CenterY() + ((Object2.CenterY() - Object1.CenterY()) / max(0.001, CuDist)) * MaxDistance) - Object2.CenterY()) * Strength
((Object1.Object3D::CenterZ() + ((Object2.Object3D1::CenterZ() - Object1.Object3D::CenterZ()) / max(0.001, CuDist)) * MaxDistance) - Object2.Object3D1::CenterZ()) * Strength
((Object1.CenterY() + ((Object2.CenterY() - Object1.CenterY()) / max(0.001, CuDist)) * MaxDistance) - Object2.CenterY()) * Strength

parameters.description: The physics object to be constrained.
parameters.name: Object2

parameters.description: The maximum cone angle (0-360°) from Object1's forward face.
parameters.name: AngleLimit

parameters.description: How "snappy" the correction is. Higher values act like stiff springs; lower values act like rubber bands.
parameters.name: Strength

eventsFunctions.description: Checks if camera is looking towards an object withing an angle limit (or field of view). This accounts for both the 2D angle and the 3D Y-axis rotation (pitch).
eventsFunctions.fullName: Camera Is looking towards an Object
eventsFunctions.name: CameraIsLooking
eventsFunctions.sentence: Camera is looking at _PARAM1_ (within _PARAM3_°)

parameters: sqrt(pow(CameraCenterX() - Object2.CenterX(), 2) + pow(CameraCenterY() - Object2.CenterY(), 2) + pow(Scene3D::CameraZ() - Object2.Object3D1::CenterZ(), 2))
Object1.CenterX() + (fwdx * cos(ToRad(FieldOfView)) + (dX - fwdx * dt) / max(0.0001, sin(a)) * sin(ToRad(FieldOfView))) * CuDist
Object1.CenterY() + (fwdy * cos(ToRad(FieldOfView)) + (dy - fwdy * dt) / max(0.0001, sin(a)) * sin(ToRad(FieldOfView))) * CuDist

parameters.description: The half-angle of the vision cone (e.g., 45° creates a 90° total FOV).
parameters.name: FieldOfView

eventsFunctions.description: Changes Objects Position Relative to an 3D coordenate and Camera
eventsFunctions.name: OverlayNonScale
eventsFunctions.sentence: Project object _PARAM1_ to 3D position X: _PARAM2_, Y: _PARAM3_, Z: _PARAM4_ (Offsets: _PARAM5_, _PARAM6_, _PARAM7_). Hide if off-screen? _PARAM8_

parameters: FP3D::Overlay(X + OffsetX, Y + OffsetY, Z + OffsetZ, "X", "yes")
FP3D::Overlay(X + OffsetX, Y + OffsetY, Z + OffsetZ, "Y", "yes")
FP3D::Overlay(X + OffsetX, Y + OffsetY, Z + OffsetZ, "X", "no")
FP3D::Overlay(X + OffsetX, Y + OffsetY, Z + OffsetZ, "Y", "no")

eventsFunctions.description: gets the normals Y and X and gives the angle based on that
eventsFunctions.name: NormalToAngle

eventsFunctions.description: Calculates all normals and checks if  and gives you the elevation angle based on that.
eventsFunctions.fullName: Gets the Elevation angle from normals
eventsFunctions.name: NormalToElevation

eventsBasedBehaviors.description: Automatically handles the complex math required to attach 3D objects (viewmodels, weapons, items) to the player's 3D camera. It supports linear and angular interpolation (lerping) for realistic weapon "weight" and sway.
eventsBasedBehaviors.name: FPSObjectParenting

eventsFunctions.description: Chrcks the current vertical offset.
eventsFunctions.name: VerticalOffset

eventsFunctions.description: Horizontal position (X-axis) relative to the camera.
eventsFunctions.name: HorizontalOffset

eventsFunctions.description: Correction for the base horizontal angle.
eventsFunctions.name: AngleOffset

eventsFunctions.description: Correction for the base vertical angle.
eventsFunctions.name: ElevationOffset

eventsFunctions.description: Additional elevation adjustment on the X-axis.
eventsFunctions.name: XElevationOffset

propertyDescriptors.label: Vertical Position
propertyDescriptors.description: Sets the vertical (Y-axis) displacement relative to the camera center. Negative values move the object down.
propertyDescriptors.name: VerticalOffset

propertyDescriptors.label: Horizontal Position
propertyDescriptors.description: Sets the horizontal (X-axis) displacement relative to the camera center. Positive values move the object to the right.
propertyDescriptors.name: HorizontalOffset

propertyDescriptors.label: Follow Camera Rotation
propertyDescriptors.description: When enabled, the object will inherit the camera's Yaw, Pitch, and Roll. If disabled, it stays at a fixed angle.
propertyDescriptors.name: Rotate

propertyDescriptors.label: Yaw Offset
propertyDescriptors.description: Adds a base horizontal rotation (in degrees) to the object. Useful for correcting the model's forward facing.
propertyDescriptors.name: AngleOffset

propertyDescriptors.label: Pitch Offset
propertyDescriptors.description: Adds a base vertical rotation (in degrees). Use this to tilt the weapon or item up or down.
propertyDescriptors.name: ElevationOffset

propertyDescriptors.label: Movement Smoothness
propertyDescriptors.description: Controls the linear interpolation of the position. A value of 0 is instant; 0.7 adds a smooth trailing effect (Sway).
propertyDescriptors.name: Lerping

propertyDescriptors.label: Forward Distance
propertyDescriptors.description: Sets how far in front of the camera the object is placed (Z-axis). Use negative values to move it further away.
propertyDescriptors.name: Distance

propertyDescriptors.label: Rotation Smoothness
propertyDescriptors.description: Controls the interpolation of the rotation. Higher values make the object turn more slowly than the camera (Angular Sway).
propertyDescriptors.name: AngularLerping

propertyDescriptors.label: Roll Offset
propertyDescriptors.description: Adds a base tilt adjustment on the X-axis (Roll). Useful for "gangsta style" weapon holds or leaning effects.
propertyDescriptors.name: XElevation

propertyDescriptors.label: Inherit Pitch (Y)
propertyDescriptors.description: Toggles whether the object follows the camera when looking up or down. Usually set to "Yes" for FPS weapons.
propertyDescriptors.name: EnableYRot

propertyDescriptors.label: Inherit Roll (X)
propertyDescriptors.description: Toggles whether the object follows the camera's tilt. Setting this to "No" keeps the item vertically stable.
propertyDescriptors.name: EnableXRot

eventsBasedBehaviors.description: Automates procedural animations for viewmodels (like weapons). It uses mathematical oscillators to simulate walking movement and provides interpolation actions to smoothly transition between states, such as moving from a "Hip-fire" position to an "Aim-Down-Sights" (ADS) position.
eventsBasedBehaviors.name: FpsAnimation

eventsFunctions.description: Creates a procedural looping movement by oscillating the horizontal and vertical offsets of an object. It uses sine waves to simulate the natural up-and-down, side-to-side motion of a character walking or breathing.
eventsFunctions.name: WalkAnimation
eventsFunctions.sentence: Apply walk animation to _PARAM0_: vertical distance _PARAM2_ (speed _PARAM4_), horizontal distance _PARAM3_ (speed _PARAM5_)

parameters.description: How many pixels/units the object moves up and down from its base position.
parameters.name: VerticalDistance

parameters.description: How many pixels/units the object moves left and right.
parameters.name: HorizontalDistance

parameters.description: The frequency of the vertical movement. Higher = faster bobbing.
parameters.name: VerticalTime

parameters.description: The frequency of the horizontal movement.
parameters.name: HorizontalTime

eventsFunctions.description: Smoothly rotates an object's local offsets (Yaw, Pitch, and Roll) toward target values. This is primarily used to simulate recoil, weapon sway when turning, or "leaning" mechanics in a first-person perspective.
eventsFunctions.name: InterpolateAngles
eventsFunctions.sentence: Interpolate angles of _PARAM0_: Angle _PARAM2_, Elevation _PARAM3_, X-Elevation _PARAM4_ with lerp _PARAM5_

parameters.description: Target Yaw: The horizontal rotation goal.
parameters.name: offsetAngle

parameters.description: Target Pitch: The vertical tilt goal.
parameters.name: offsetElevation

parameters.description: Target Roll: The side-to-side tilt goal.
parameters.name: offsetElevationX

parameters.description: Speed: 0 is instant, 0.1 is smooth, 0.01 is very heavy/slow.
parameters.name: lerp

eventsFunctions.description: Smoothly transitions the object's local offsets (Horizontal, Vertical, and Distance) towards new target values. This is the primary tool for creating "Aim Down Sights" (ADS) transitions, weapon drawing/holstering animations, and sprinting offsets.
eventsFunctions.name: InterpolatePositions
eventsFunctions.sentence: Interpolate positions of _PARAM0_: Horizontal _PARAM2_, Vertical _PARAM3_, Distance _PARAM4_ with lerp _PARAM5_

parameters.description: Target X: The side-to-side goal (0 is center).
parameters.name: HorizontalOffset

parameters.description: Speed: Smoothing factor (e.g., 0.1 for smooth, 0.3 for snappy).
parameters.name: lerp

eventsBasedBehaviors.description: make the object snap to an grid
eventsBasedBehaviors.name: SnapToGrid
